channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ecuador's incumbent president and a leftist lawyer again vie for the top job in election runoff
item.description: QUITO, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuadorians are voting Sunday in the country’s presidential election runoff, facing the choice between incumbent President Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Convalescing Pope Francis opens Holy Week with in-person greeting in St. Peter's Square
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A convalescing Pope Francis greeted the crowd in St. Peter’s Square on Palm Sunday, wishing more than 20,000 faithful a “Happy Palm Sunday, Happy Holy Week," in yet another reassuring public sign of his recovery from a life-threatening battle with double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fears of another narco-state rise as Latin American country readies for pivotal vote
item.description: Ecuador is headed to a second round of a presidential election in which incumbent President Daniel Noboa faces leftist challenger Luisa González as the nation faces gang violence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens are reported killed in east Congo as the government and Rwanda-backed rebels trade blame
item.description: Congolese authorities say at least 50 people were killed in weekend attacks in the country's conflict-hit east where government forces are fighting Rwanda-backed rebels.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kabumba And Jean-Yves Kamale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian missiles hit Ukrainian city of Sumy during Palm Sunday celebrations, killing more than 30
item.description: Russian missiles struck the heart of the Ukrainian city of Sumy as people gathered to celebrate Palm Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Central Myanmar shaken by new quake in one of the biggest aftershocks since March 28 temblor
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A magnitude 5.5 earthquake struck on Sunday morning near Meiktila, a small city in central Myanmar, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump team holds 'constructive' face-to-face nuclear talks with Iran, will meet again next weekend
item.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare face-to-face nuclear talks in Oman Saturday. The Trump administration is pushing for diplomacy backed by military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tragic New York City helicopter crash hits home in Barcelona where victims were well known
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — As they sat inside a helicopter shortly before a flight over the towering skyscrapers of New York City, Agustin Escobar flashed a thumbs-up while his wife and children beamed big smiles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic on Saturday demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade on Friday, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's divisions on display as presidential candidates bicker in a live TV debate
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Bickering and chaos overshadowed a presidential debate in Poland on Friday evening as eight candidates met to discuss existential matters for this country on NATO's eastern flank — including fears of Russia over its war in Ukraine and concerns about President Donald Trump’s com
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: Prince Harry is touching down in Ukraine, getting a first-hand look at a rehabilitation center in the war-torn country. The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: In a turbulent time, Turkey lays claim to a role of stability for Europe, Syria and global trade
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday sought to position his country as a point of stability in an increasingly tumultuous world, saying Turkey was essential for European security and able to span geopolitical divisions over Ukraine, Syria and U.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Mexico with additional tariffs in water dispute
item.description: President Trump late Thursday threatened to impose additional tariffs and potential sanctions against Mexico over a long-running water dispute related to a 1944 treaty.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Panama and US lock in new security pact for Canal as China tensions simmer
item.description: Panama and the U.S. signed a new security pact on the canal, boosting defense ties as tensions with China over Latin American influence continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Senate approves Peter Hoekstra as next US ambassador to Canada
item.description: The Senate approved Peter Hoekstra to be the next U.S. ambassador to Canada amid one of the toughest times in Washington's relationship with Ottawa as the tariff war heats up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee as Trump's ambassador to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted Wednesday to confirm former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee as the Trump administration’s ambassador to Israel, two days after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's White House visit.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida men charged in 'truly heinous' global child porn ring involving over 1M files
item.description: The Florida Department of Law Enforcement arrest seven people in Florida for an international child pornography ring involving over one million files purchased through social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slain Hamas hostage's family fights for the release of those still in Gaza
item.description: Oded Lifshitz, an 83-year-old great-grandfather, returned to Israel in a coffin more than 500 days after he was taken from his home. Now, his family is fighting for the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 dead, 120 injured in Dominican Republican nightclub rooftop collapse
item.description: 18 people died and more than 120 were injured when a roof fell at a nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
